meta.rule: Every prose-writing agent writes a same-named .kg.json alongside its result .md.
meta.why: The KG reflects exactly what agents wrote (no LLM inference), so the byproduct file is the single source of truth.
meta.when: After every output from paper-summarizer / answer-formulator / critic / experiment-planner / code-implementer / results-analyst.

nodes.name: paper-hunt keywords match title OR abstract

meta.rule: paper-hunter matches keywords against both title and abstract across all sources (arxiv / anthology / openreview). arxiv queries use `(ti:"kw" OR abs:"kw")`; anthology fetches detail without a title pre-filter at listing and then matches the title+abstract string; openreview dumps the full venueid so both are automatically covered.
meta.why: The previous arxiv `abs:"kw"`-only / anthology title-only pre-filter scheme structurally missed papers where the keyword appeared on only one side. As the recall stage, a miss in paper-hunter is unrecoverable — downstream triage never sees it.
meta.when: Preserve this rule when editing paper-hunt code. Anthology detail fetches are expensive, but the `--max-per-venue-year` cap cuts off fetches as soon as the cap is reached, so do not reintroduce a title pre-filter.

nodes.name: paper-summarizer does NOT read PDFs — Gemini digest 2-stage pipeline assumed

meta.rule: Do not estimate the paper-summarizer Claude-side cost as 'Claude reads the full PDF'. The real pipeline is Stage 1 (Gemini CLI gemini-3-pro-preview 1M context reads the PDF and produces a 2-5k word dense Markdown digest, cached at papers/digest/<slug>.digest.md) -> Stage 2 (Claude reads digest + raw.md metadata only and writes the 5-part Marp + Critical Reading + Known/Unknown + .kg.json). Claude cost per paper is digest ~3k tokens + raw.md metadata read + Marp template fill + Critical Reading inference ≈ 30-60 seconds. A 176-paper run fits in ~2h, and detouring via a template batch script (e.g. a3_batch_summarize.py) piles abstract-only / heuristic summaries into normal venue directories, which pollutes the downstream QA stage evidence chain.
meta.why: During 2026-04-16 diffusion-LLM corpus collection, the orchestrator mis-modeled paper-summarizer as 'Claude reads full PDF ~3 min per paper', concluded 176 × 3 min = 8.75h was infeasible, attempted to write a template batch Python driver, and halted on user denial. In reality the digest is already produced by Gemini, so Claude does not read the PDF directly — it only consumes the digest. This was already documented (.claude/skills/paper-summarize/SKILL.md §17-52 and .claude/agents/paper-summarizer.md §24-27), but the orchestrator ignored those and mis-budgeted time. Taking the batch-template detour would degrade Critical Reading to the digest's 'Limitations stated by authors', losing the author-claim/actual-evidence separation, after which QA answer-formulator would produce incorrect evidence chains.
meta.when: Before dispatching A-3 paper-summarizer the orchestrator must always: (1) check digest cache presence ratio (count under papers/digest/), (2) handle remaining digest generation in a separate background bash, (3) estimate Claude work as '30-60s per paper × count', (4) if a single agent turn is insufficient, parallel-dispatch paper-summarizer across partitions of the accepted list, (5) do NOT pile heuristic templates via a batch driver — the skill's pymupdf fallback (paper-summarize §60-67) handles Gemini OOM papers. Any new Gemini heap (NODE_OPTIONS) tuning lives in scripts/gemini_digest.py only.
